name: Odiorne Point State Park (+ Seacoast Science Ctr)
Wickford village
details: Tiny harbor village, duck-feeding, kayak launch, penny-candy shop feel.

name: Wakefield / Kingston
details: Old Mountain Field playground, ice cream, easy small-town wandering near the beaches.

name: Tiverton Four Corners
details: Gray's Ice Cream, art barns, farm stands - a short, sweet stop.

name: Aquidneck Island (Newport/Middletown/Portsmouth)
details: The whole island: Newport harbor, Middletown beaches + Norman Bird, Portsmouth's Green Animals + railbike + big playground. Easy to base a whole-day loop here.

name: Newport (waterfront, Bowen's Wharf, harbor)
details: Boats & gulls, harbor seals sometimes, ice cream, climb on the rocks at King Park; pair with Green Animals topiary or Cliff Walk.

name: Duxbury (beach + Powder Point Bridge)
details: Walk the long wooden Powder Point Bridge, tide flats with crabs, calm beach.

name: Westerly / Watch Hill (Flying Horse Carousel)
details: Ride the antique Flying Horse Carousel, beach, Taylor Swift's house from afar, ice cream.

name: Boston (Common, Greenway, Freedom Trail)
details: Frog Pond + carousel + the Greenway's giant rings fountain kids run through; ducklings statues (Make Way for Ducklings); swan boats.

name: SoWa Open Market
details: Food trucks, street art, makers - easy graze-and-wander on a Sunday.

name: Mystic (drawbridge, Olde Mistick Village)
details: Watch the bascule bridge lift, ducks at the village pond, ice cream, pair with the aquarium or seaport.

name: Marblehead (harbor + tide-pool islands)
details: At low tide walk out to Crowninshield & Gerry Islands; lots of crabs and snails in the coves.

name: Martha's Vineyard (Oak Bluffs, Edgartown)
details: Flying Horses Carousel (oldest in US, grab the brass ring), gingerbread cottages, beaches, ferry ride is half the fun.

name: Rockport (Bearskin Neck)
details: Artsy peninsula of shops + the famous red fishing shack (Motif #1) + tide pools at Old Garden Beach.

name: Salem (waterfront, witch lore, museums)
details: Pirate museum, tall ship, friendly-spooky vibe, harbor; not-too-scary for a 5yo if you skip the gory exhibits.

name: York (Nubble Light, York's Wild Kingdom)
details: Nubble Lighthouse photo op + a small zoo/amusement park (York's Wild Kingdom).

name: Block Island (Mohegan Bluffs, beaches)
details: Ferry adventure + the 140-step staircase down the bluffs + beaches + bike/moped the island.

name: Provincetown / Cape Cod towns
details: Whale-watch boats leave from here, dunes to run, taffy shops, Pilgrim Monument to climb.

name: Nantucket
details: Cobblestone streets, ice cream, whaling museum, beaches, ferry ride. Bikeable, walkable, very kid-doable as a day trip from Hyannis.

name: Ogunquit (Marginal Way, Perkins Cove)
details: The Marginal Way cliff path + drawbridge at Perkins Cove + wide sandy beach.

name: Historic Deerfield
details: Costumed history, open fields, hands-on colonial crafts on event days.

name: Way Way Store (historic general store)
details: Old-time penny-candy stop if you're passing through southern Maine.

name: Kennebunkport
details: Trolley museum nearby, Dock Square, beaches, lobster-boat watching.

name: Portland (Old Port, ferries, lighthouses)
details: Cobblestone streets, fire-boat & ferries, narrow-gauge railway rides, lighthouse at Fort Williams park.

name: The Berkshires (region)
details: Base camp for mountain coasters, sculpture parks, gardens, swimming holes - good multi-day hub.

name: Rockland (breakwater lighthouse walk)
details: Walk the nearly-mile-long granite breakwater out to the lighthouse - a real adventure for a 5yo.

name: Woodstock (covered bridge, Billings Farm)
details: Covered bridge + Billings Farm (cows, wagon rides, butter-making) - very hands-on for little kids.

name: Burlington (Church St, Lake Champlain)
details: Pedestrian street performers, ECHO science center on the lake, ferry rides, splash fountains.

name: Bar Harbor (gateway to Acadia)
details: Tide pools at low tide, carriage roads, popovers, Cadillac Mtn sunrise; sandbar walk to Bar Island at low tide.

name: Chesterwood
priceNote: ~$20 adult; under 12 free; sculptor's studio + grounds

name: Rainforest Cafe (themed dining)
details: Location TBD - verify nearest Rainforest Cafe (Providence Place Mall historically).

name: Plymouth, MA
details: Plymouth Rock, Mayflower II ship to board, waterfront, Plimoth Patuxet living history nearby; very kid-graspable history.

name: Manchester-by-the-Sea, MA
details: Singing Beach (sand actually squeaks!), harbor, ice cream; classic North Shore beach-town day.

name: Exeter, NH
details: Pretty riverfront downtown, bandstand, swan-filled river, ice cream, Water St shops; gentle small-town wander.

name: South Berwick, ME
details: Storybook small town (Hamilton House + Vaughan Woods trails along the river); quiet, scenic, low-key.

name: Eliot, ME / Stratham, NH (Seacoast border towns)
details: Quiet Seacoast towns near Portsmouth; pair with Stratham Hill Park playground + nearby Alnoba/sculpture. Add-on to a Portsmouth day.
